title: Start research for a new disease
intro: One click fans out six parallel workstreams: PubMed mining and the long guideline draft, plus five fast finders that pull specialist doctors, recruiting clinical trials, therapies, patient foundations and the recognised consensus paper. Each workstream writes its results directly to the disease page as it lands — you don't need to wait for the whole run to finish.
workstreamGuidelineSub: PubMed mining → therapy + diagnostic extraction → drafted sections with PMID citations
workstreamFindersLabel: Five fast finders (~45 s each, in parallel)
workstreamFindersSub: Doctors · clinical trials · therapies · patient foundations · recognised consensus paper
privacyNote: We don't store patient data — only the disease name and the research criteria. You will receive a public link to the run progress page.
fieldHint: Search by canonical name, alternative names, gene symbol, OMIM or Orphanet ID. If the disease is not yet in our catalogue, we will help identify it and launch the research.
consentPrefix: I understand the AI output is an
consentSuffix: — a source-cited summary that no one has officially verified or signed off — and it does not replace medical advice; I should read it with a clinician.
errorUnauthorized: The server rejected the request (401). Set VITE_GENEGUIDELINES_API_KEY when the backend has its API gate enabled, or run jobs from the operator console.
errorQueueFull: You already have several runs in the queue — wait for one to finish before starting another.
errorGeneric: Could not start the research run.
submitPickDisease: Pick a disease from the list
symptomComingSoon: Symptom-based search is coming soon. For now, please type a disease name.
